section_title: INTRODUCTION AND WEEKLY DOLLAR INDEX OVERVIEW
content:
Good morning folks. This session will be brief as I want to return to watching live charts with my youngest
son here.
Recalling from the month of August, if your notes are being maintained well, we were looking for price to
run above the buy side here on the daily chart, eventually drawing here. I said if we got animated above
this level, our attention should be focused on this inefficiency. In your charts, you should have this
highlighted and drawn out into the future. This is all weekly level, very very high time frame.
It's going to take a little bit of time to get to these types of levels. Whenever I refer to weekly targets like
this, invariably someone new or uninterested in learning will take it out of context, expecting that level to
happen that very moment or that trading day. This is not the case; it is to keep you focused on where my
mind is, the internal dialogue as I'm watching price each day, each session, each time I watch price present
a setup. You can trade against this once you get better versed in reading price action. In the beginning, it
should be something you submit to, only looking for setups in these long-term directional draws on liquidity.
That's the primary function of how I teach in the beginning.

section_title: HIGH TIME FRAME BIAS AND TRADING PSYCHOLOGY
content:
It takes a little bit of time to get warmed up to the idea, especially if you're looking at lower time frame
charts (we'll drop down to the daily now). It's easy to get lulled into thinking that a long-term target might
not be where price is going, or to oppose these longer term draws on liquidity, which can lead to trouble,
regret, and remorse. You'll feel the pain and agony of knowing you did something I've told you many times
to avoid in long drawn out Woodshed moments in these videos and in the Twitter spaces (which is primarily
new trade psychology).
It's better hearing it versus me trying to type it up into a book. When you hear someone beat it in your head
to focus on these things, not other things, and how you're supposed to be navigating these problem areas
not to be afraid of them but to tackle them that's what the Twitter spaces are for. They're raw,
sometimes not using eloquent language, but I don't promise eloquence.
The idea is drawing towards the low end of that weekly sell side imbalance buy side inefficiency.
Remember, on the weekly chart, this is one single down-closed candle, but now it's two. Buy side resides
just above here, on the week of November 21st, 2022. That high comes in at 107.993, so we'll just call 108
as the long-term buy side liquidity. We have a smaller one here where this high comes in at 107.95, which
is essentially in agreement that if it takes this high here on November 30th, the week of November 30th, we
take that high and chances are it's going to run and touch the bottom of that weekly sell side imbalance
buy side inefficiency. These are the levels you should have on your chart from the high time frame weekly
and daily.

- WEEKLY AND DAILY TARGETS
We'll drop into a Euro chart real quick to satisfy the old FX group. We've been watching the Euro and Cable
in dollar. I know some of you want me to drop into these lower time frame charts because you want to pass
your funded account challenges and stuff, but I've been telling you where the market's been going on the
daily chart and the weekly chart.
It might be one of those things where you can see it now panning out like I outlined, and you're regretting
that you didn't do something with the information, or worked with this as your bias looking for setups. Don't
beat yourself up; don't vent on social media to me about why you didn't listen or anything like that. That's
the worst thing to do. Don't complain in your little circles on Discord or Telegram channels or other Twitter
communities. By doing that, you're reinforcing bad habits and reinforcing toxic thinking.
If you didn't do anything with this information, if you weren't looking for shorts like we were outlining, that
would be the best case scenario because dollars bullish, Euro should be bearish, the Pound Dollar should
be bearish. There was news last night by the way for Aussie dollar; I completely forgot to mention it. I don't
usually bring it up, but I'm going to look at that one next. But without having looked, I don't know, but Aussie
dollar should have went lower. I don't know if it did, but I knew it had some news like in the evening time my
local time, so I'll take a look at that in a moment. But we're going to go through Euro first, then Pound
Dollar, then Aussie Dollar. I'll look at gold real quick and then we'll go into the index features and then I'll
wrap it up because I got to get busy with my youngest son here.
So anyway, we're looking at this draw on liquidity here. I mentioned how this inefficiency would act as an
inversion fair value gap. That's not noted now, but go back and don't take my word for anything I'm saying
here. Go back and look at the analysis that I gave in Twitter spaces talking about it, in the live streams that
I've talked about these things, in the video commentaries that I've posted. Incidentally, I also have this in my
notes here. I saw a tweet someone mentioned that there was not a tweet, I'm sorry, I get a lot of
comments in my videos and somebody mentioned a link to me that is a YouTube video for my 2023
playlist, but I was unable to put it in the playlist. I don't know how I messed that up, but if you're listening or
if you know that there should be a video that's not in the 2023 mentorship playlist, send that link to me on
Twitter so that way I can make sure it's in the playlist. I appreciate that.

We were looking for lower prices, drawn liquidity here, and I said if we get animated below this, we will be
moving over to these lows here. So the first one's here, and that's what we're having highlighted with this
weekly candle on the week of March 13th, 2023. That low comes in at 1.03162. So we will be expecting the
price to draw eventually down into that. I'm not saying that it's going to happen today; it could happen
sometime the rest of this week. It just gives you a framework to aim for so that way you're not getting
caught up in the excitement of lower time frame charts and completely forgetting that this is where the likely
algorithm is pulling price to. I'll drop down the daily chart real quick, and no, I'm not dropping any lower than
a daily chart. You don't need that. Yes we do, I T, you're holding it back.
All right, so there is where I believe we're going to draw to. It's enough to get something out of it. Should it
give you another opportunity today or going into the Friday? We talked about this level here in consequent
encroachment. Look at the bodies. Go back and listen to all that jawboning session. We tanked here,
created another sell side imbalance buy side inefficiency on this candle here on September 5th. So we
traded up into that. That right there is a swing trade for those of you that don't have the ability to sit and
watch day trades. You don't want to trade five-minute chart. You don't want to trade anything less than a
daily time frame because you have a work schedule or you're just a working class here, you're running a
business, you're in school, you have to sleep, and your life doesn't allow you to do these types of lower
intraday setups.

section_title: PRICE FRACTAL NATURE AND HIGH TIME FRAME IMPORTANCE
content:
The formations in price action on the daily chart are doing the same things that you see me and my
students trading with one-minute charts and 30-second charts, 15-second charts, five-second charts, and
one-second charts. Price is fractal, meaning that everything that I use to get into a time frame of any time
frame, of any market, is applicable for any time frame that you can use that fits your specific model.
If these lower time frame charts are moving too fast (I say that in a manner that is tongue-in-cheek,
meaning that they're not going any faster than daily charts moving), you're afraid. You're building this fear
that these markets are moving supersonic in the lower time frames when they're moving at the same exact
pace. The problem is is you're over-leveraging many times on these lower time frames, versus just simply
doing the least in terms of leverage and using the experience that multiple time frame entries on lower time
frame charts can provide you. It just gives you lots of opportunity to practice or backtest, but never losing
sight of these higher time frame charts because this is where the real draw by institutional investors,
traders, large firms they're doing their analysis on the weekly and daily. That's why I've been talking to
you in these time frames, because your mindset needs to be anchored on these time frames
predominantly, and then using the information that's derived from these time frames, then engaging in
being a mechanic with price action in the lower time frame charts with this logic in mind.

If you do those types of things, your win rate will increase. You will still suffer losing trades. I'm not trying to
promote the idea that you can trade perfectly. I take losses; you've seen them. You've seen me take them.
You've seen me work out of them. You see me reverse on them after being stopped out, even being right or
wrong. It doesn't change the model. It doesn't remove the efficacy of it; it just makes the reality of making
and losing money what it is. And if you have your logic as to what you're aiming for, what you're trying to
frame the trades on not for pattern's sake, not for team mentality sake, meaning you know supply and
demand, Elliott wave, Wyckoff, you know whatever. And by the way, Steve Morrow, I didn't copy that stuff
from him. All his stuff is from my 1996 notes, so stop leaving comments in my video channel. Steve
Tomorrow is welcome to come out here and do whatever he thinks he can do live, and I'll work circles
around him too. Yes, that was an ego, and that's me beating my chest. He's a student, that's the way it is.
This right here, that inefficiency trading right into that, that is a swing setup. That's a swing trade. And what
would you be aiming for? You'd be aiming for the run below that low here. In longer term, you would leave
your stop loss above this high. This is what makes long-term swing trading very difficult because you have
to allow the market to go into profit and then be willing to see it go back against you. So as a long-term
swing trader, for those that are listening, if you want to have some notes on this, I'll talk more about it in my
books, and you can go back and look at my YouTube core content lessons around swing trading. In swing
trading, you want to try to build in a position slow. And when it gets to a higher time frame discount, if you're
short like we're seeing here, this is where you would take some of the short off, waiting for it to potentially
give you another reversal so you can add it back, and that would be inside of this area right in here. There's
your fair value gap, and inside that fair value gap, your stop would be above here, never moving it. You
cannot move it. You have to leave it there. And that's scary. That's why swing trading, you think that you're
avoiding risk by not trying to get in these lower time frame charts, when that's the best place for you to
learn, because it'll teach you stop place management, it'll teach you patience, it'll teach you no fear of
running out of setups, it'll teach you that your model works. It'll give you more in the trenches experience of
reading price real time than any other time frame.

But in swing trading, if you're trading on a daily chart, you cannot make a move of moving that stop loss to
break even, trailing it to lock in a profitable exit. All you're doing is ensuring that you're going to get stopped
out prematurely before your objective is met, because there's a lot of give and take on a daily time frame
unless that you have timed your entry, which you're not going to do this in the beginning, it's going to take
you years. So when you hear me talk about it's going to take you years to get good at this, getting good at it
is not profitable. I've already said that you can be profitable rather quickly if you do the right things and
study the right way, but to be very good at it where you can time the very explosive type of price action runs
that start right from when you try to get in at, that takes years of getting in experience to do that.
So in the beginning, you have to give yourself the permission and the time to build up your experience so
that way you're not holding yourself to a level of expectation that's unrealistic or unreasonable. And then
when you don't hit it, you start doing things recklessly overtrading, over-leveraging, trading outside of
your model because you're impulsive. You just want to fix the mistake you just made. You want to get back
that loss that you just took. You want to fix the drawdown. You all these types of things that's how it
creeps into your trading. So on swing trading, in this instance here, if you're watching price, you would see
this fair value gap here on the week of September 5th, knowing that it trades up into that. That's a setup.
You can get short there as a trader that doesn't use intraday price action. The way you would do this is on
this... (zoom in).
Also, trading on a daily chart like this means your leverage is going to be extremely low. I know you want to
trade with whatever the maximum these funded account challenges and firm companies will offer you, but
you got to stop thinking like that. Because if you've been watching my son's Top Step Express funded
account (I think I said that correctly, if I did not, I apologize), he's only traded one contract, and now he's
being offered the ability to trade with five. He won't be trading with five if he's going to stay out of the
Woodshed with me. He's just going to do one contract through the duration until he gets the ten thousand
dollars and then he's done.

section_title: SWING TRADING ENTRY AND STOP ORDER STRATEGY
content:
Trading with a daily chart, you want to trade with the lowest leverage that'll allow you and afford you the
ability to trade with an idea of selling weakness as a swing trader. If you can't watch intraday price action,
that means you absolutely must one of two things:
- Either get up during the day and look at London, and wait for a run that would be, in other words, in this
area here when it was trading up into this place upon here. Now, I know some of you may be watching this
for the first time. This is all hindsight. It's all hindsight. No, it's not. We talked about these things before it
even happened. We talked about price being here. We talked about the price going down here, and it's
happening on your daily charts. I'm not bending time and space; I'm literally telling you the future using the
concepts that I've laid in your hands for free.
- But you have to do one of two things: you have to get up on the day that you want to try to enter on your
swing trade, which would be here, here, or here. Any one of those days, and you have to allow for a stop
loss that's above this high right there, because you're swing trading. And you would sell a run below this
day's (September 12th) opening price. You can use the settlement price here on TradingView. In my
opinion, I would be using the midnight opening price in New York local time. If it trades below that, you'd be
going selling short. You would sell weakness as it breaks away from that inefficiency and goes below this
candle's low. So your trading wouldn't really begin until this day here because the open on this candle is
down here, which makes no sense, right? Because you're too far away from the fair value gap. This is the
first day that sets you up as a non-intraday, non-minute, second, 15-minute, one-hour, four-hour, anything
less than a daily chart you can't enter on because your life is what it is, and there's no shame in that. It
doesn't mean you can't make money; it just means you're going to trade with a whole lot more patience
required, a whole lot less leverage, and your stop losses are going to be absolutely enormous compared to
what you see me using. Are you willing to compromise? If it's all about making money and making ends
meet, and moving towards a better lifestyle through another source of income that's potentially available to
you but not guaranteed to anyone, then that means that it won't matter that this is the way you're going to
have to do it. But the logic is still the same, because if this was a five-minute chart or if this is a 15-second
chart and this was the fair value gap, I would tell you that the stop loss needs to be above here anyway,
because this is the candle that makes the inefficiency. So your stop loss has to be above here. Well, I can't
trade that way. Okay, trade something else. Don't use my logic. Go out there and lose money. Go do
whatever you want to do. But if you're asking me how to teach it to you and how it should be done, that's
not in an intraday capacity. This is what you're going to do.
Because all you need to know is whatever this opening price is here, or whatever the opening price is at
midnight that day, in the next trading day here, wait until after 9:00 PM the next day. Why? Because now
you have Asian session in place. If it trades below that, you're short. Your stop goes above here. Whatever
the leverage is required to allow you to do that and not risk more than whatever you're comfortable with
never more than one percent. That's a big stop loss, blah blah, but this is a big run down here. Think. You
have to start somewhere, and if you can't start in the intraday charts and you can only operate in this until
you make life changes (because you may have to do like, if you want to be a trader, if you really want to be
a trader and you can find your way of making consistently accurate setups even in this manner), you will
make changes to allow you to trade in the lower time frame charts, guaranteed. No one's going to say, 'No,
I don't want to make a thousand dollars a day when I'm stuck making two thousand dollars over the course
of eight weeks, four weeks, or whatever it is.' Believe me, money talks and walks. You will absolutely make
changes in your life. You'll convince your spouse and then start seeing the money. That's the way it works.
Nobody's going to believe you in the beginning. When you start showing the receipts and the money,
they're all of a sudden they're interested in what you have to say about it. But in the beginning, you don't
have that, so you have to keep quiet about what you're doing. Don't tell your left hand what your right hand
is doing.
But once this setup here forms in this day here, you'd be selling short on weakness. Guess what that
means? That means you can sell short on a stop. That means you're putting a stop loss that would put you
in the trade. It's not stopping you out with a loss; it's a sales stop at this candle's or that day's opening price,
preferably midnight. I use midnight New York local time. I like that. So as long as price on the next day was
above it here, who cares? You're not interested in that, and you're sleeping. You're at work, you're at
school, you're doing whatever. At some point, it's going to trade below that, that level here, and that stop
order will become a market order to sell short, and then your open order for shorts needs to be protected
with a stop loss above here.

section_title: FIBONACCI SETTINGS AND IMPLIED DEALING RANGE
content:
This will be a first partial. You can put multiple trades on, folks. Someone's thinking, 'I can't do size.' You're
right, you can't put a lot of size on it, but you can put something on here where you can take a partial.
Down here, you take off one of them. Then because we see this big run down into this low, it creates a
potential inefficiency, which is confirmed when this candle right here on September 5th, once it closes, that
high between this candle's high right here and that candle's low, we have an inefficiency. So you can take
an add inside this inefficiency. Whatever you took off as a partial down here, you can add it back when it
trades up into here. But look at this heat right here, ICT. It doesn't matter. You're anchored with your core
entry up here, and your stop still remains right there. Can you swing trade with that logic? Because if you
can't, then swing trading is not going to work for you. I'm just going to save you a whole lot of time. That is
how it is. If you do anything apart from that, you're going to get stopped out a lot. You're going to take more
losing trades. You're going to have a lot of failed launches with your setups, and you're going to be really
impatient, and all of those things are going to coalesce into a tapestry that will keep you from being
profitable, and it'll just make you want to quit. You'll either want to do something else, or you'll want to do
nothing at all with the marketplace. And that's the reality. I'm not trying to sugarcoat it for any of you; that's
the way it is. And there it is.
But you can add it back here, and then once we break below here, you can take another partial, or
between this low and the next level down here, that would be again that weekly level here. Between this low
and this low, where do you take partials at, ICT? Whenever I see two targets like this, I'd like to take that
and divide it into quarters. That's not Quarters Theory. Stop telling people in your comment section or trying
to comment in mine nonsense, saying I'm teaching Quarters Theory. Quarters Theory has absolutely
nothing to do with what I'm doing. I'm projecting an implied dealing range. That means I'm anticipating that
this low will break and reach down here. For the people that just watched this for the first time, go back and
look at what's already on this YouTube channel. I already said price was going down here a month and a
half ago. I told you this is where it's going. That's not bragging. I'm reminding folks that take things out of
context because they don't take notes. They don't listen, and they want to have selective hearing. We have
not been bullish on Euro. We have not been bullish on Cable Pound Dollar. We've been bullish on Dollar.
We've been bearish on indices after it ran a specific high. When we get to that, I'll talk to that as well. But in
this low and this low here, if you take your Fib... I don't use Fib like everybody else, okay? And these guys
that run around with this retail logic like to come around and say, 'ICT does not use a Fib,' but I'm getting
the daily highs and lows in my trades. Are you?
All right, so here is (my Fib settings). Let me put this back up here because I know some of you like to ask
me, 'What's your Fib settings?' Take a screenshot of that. Again, as a reminder, I change these two here if I
want to do like TGIF measurements. I'll do the 25, I'll change that to 0.20, because that'll give me the 20
and the 30 is this one here. So I use these two boxes here for TGIF, but I have to change the 25 to 20. So
it's 20 to 30 percent of the weekly range, which is TGIF, a retracement idea. And you can watch the video I
made on that on this YouTube channel, so it's all there for free. And the rest of these are all just the
standard deviations that I use. So with that in mind, between this low here and this low down here, as price
is up here, if it breaks this low (go back and listen to it, folks, I say these things), then if it gets animated, it
means it's going to really start to move lower. Then this is where you're going to be looking for, and overall
down here, it gets overzealous. Those were my words verbatim. If this is an implied dealing range, it means
the price hasn't been there yet. It hasn't gone down there yet. So I'm implying that price can reach down
there. That's what my analysis is suggesting to the viewers that watch my commentary. So if that's the
case, then where can I take partials if I have enough to take a partial?

section_title: BLACK SWAN EVENT AND PROFIT TAKING STRATEGY
content:
Full disclosure, I have not been trading Forex for years. I've been predominantly only in index futures. Why
are you doing that, ICT? Because I think we're going to see a Black Swan event, and there's going to be a
decoupling, and I think there's going to be a crash that's going to happen really fast, and I don't want to be
in there. So there you go for disclaimer. Seek and risk. Now you know why I'm not trading Forex. So that's
why you don't want to be over-leveraging as well.
So from this low to that low here, I've broken that up into four equal parts, which is a wonderful area to take
what? A partial here if it reaches it. A partial if it goes halfway, another partial here. So right now, you
should be about 75 percent booked in profit from anything that you've been short from up here when we
were up here. How many handles? I mean, how many pips is that? (lost track of what we're talking about
here) It's a lot, isn't it? It's 400 plus, 400 handles. We're not talking about little micro moves, okay? We're
not talking about ticks and little pipette moves. Over-leveraging with 40 contracts and losing your live
stream from everybody. But we're talking about real significant price moves.
Even if you have to go through a whole lot of acrobatics and have wide stops, if you can catch 250, 300
handle runs, those things add up over the course of whatever amount of time is required for you to decide if
it's worth it for you to invest more time, more money, more life-changing decisions where you have to
change what you're doing. Maybe it requires you to stop working the job that you're working if you want to
pursue this. It may require you to change your student schedule to take classes on days that at least make
it available for you to start trading once or twice a week with intraday price data. I'm not suggesting or trying
to convince any of you to be a day trader. I'm just saying that these are some of the things you have to
weigh out if you're going to be a speculator in these markets, because in the beginning, everybody has
their limitations, and over time, you're going to have to determine whether or not if it's worth it for you to
make significant changes. And if you have a spouse or a significant other, they're not going to understand
it, so you have to at least have something to come to the discussion table and say, 'Here's what I've been
able to do with just this. Do you believe it's worthwhile for us to make an attempt to try to make some life
changes?' It may be uncomfortable. It may require you to make less money doing whatever you're doing to
be able to pursue this if this is what your heart's in. If you want to be a trader, your results will dictate the
pace at which and the timing when you want to make those changes, if at all. Obviously, if you can't prove
that you can be successful with the content, whether it be mine or someone else's, then it goes without
saying that, you know, you got to stay where you're at and, you know, keep the bills getting paid. So, long
story short, we're looking for lower prices down here in the Euro, and if a blow-off move occurs down here
(this low there at 1.0482).

All right, so we're looking for lower prices down here in the Euro, and if a blow-off move occurs down here,
okay, so this low there at 1.0482. So there you go. That's what we're looking at for Euro. Let's go back up to
a weekly chart. If you like what I'm talking about, if this is tickling your ears, giving you everything you've
been looking for, seeing the future being foretold beforehand weeks and months before it happens, take a
second, give this live stream a thumbs up. I take that as a thank you. It costs you nothing. It's nothing
coming out of your pocket. I promise it won't hurt your ego and pride. You'll be thankful that you've learned
it. So we're on a weekly chart, and now I want to see GBP USD. I always use forex.com's data.
And Pound, it has a lot of distance before it gets down to the sell side, so I favor the Pound because Euro's
already done a whole lot of movement. If we see an acceleration to the Dollar rally, I think that we'll see
very large down-closed candles, you know, large range expansion days on Cable, and we would be looking
for this low down here. There really isn't any inefficiency apart from what we're trading in right now, and I'll
show you what I mean by that. Right here, to here. If you have not put that on your chart, it should be on
there now. That's where we're trading at now. Because we're in a deep discount relative to the higher here
and the low down here. This is where price is trading at present. Look at this range right here. See this right
here? This is a balanced price range. All of this in here (I'm going to highlight it so that we can see exactly
what I'm referring to), from here to here. All of this price action, that is a balanced price range because
price has been offered up and down, and then it left it. Where is price trading essentially at right now? Half
of this range, right? Because it's a range that has bodies; it's not inefficient. So in an inefficient range,
which is a gap for inefficiency, the midpoint is consequent encroachment. Whenever there is a range that
has the bodies that are laid across the entire range, whether it be an order block or a trading range like
this, its range is balanced. So a midpoint of that is equilibrium. So we would measure it like this: low to high,
and we're just almost touching what would be equilibrium there, and we're inside of this fair value gap. So
what does that mean? What do we do with that information? (Fibonacci settings).
We can see it potentially bounce. I'm not saying it's going to go here all the way up to here. But because we
traded into this inefficiency, there hasn't been an inefficiency at all in here. See that? There's no inefficiency
in any of that. This is the only one that exists in the price run from this low all the way up to here. This is the
only one, and we're trading in it right now. So we could see it pause, pull back up into this inefficiency. I'd
like to see if it does. If it does trade up into this one here (between this candle's low and this weekly high), if
it were to trade up into that, I would like to see some of that stay open. So if, for whatever reason, it starts
to trade higher, you know, going into the end of the week or going into the next week, I would like to see
this portion stay open. Let me highlight it for you. Be careful because this week is still trading, and this high
could be taken out if it wants to retrace from the time I'm talking right now to Friday's close. So the high
that's being highlighted to make the low of this gap could change, but you're always watching price, so you
have to be aware of all that. But I'd like to see at least half of it, which would be right here. So (highlighting
portion), like this portion of it there, I would not want to see price trade up into that on a closing basis on
any daily chart at all. If it does that and it stays open like this, my mindset is we're going down here. We're
going down this (level) down here.

So, longer term, as I mentioned, you know, a month ago, that my mindset was the quarterly shift was
bearish. We wanted to see higher prices for Dollar, which is what risk-off. Risk-off means that equities,
stocks, gold, index futures, and Forex will go where? Down. You have to, you have to have this in the
beginning if you're brand new. If you don't know what you're doing, if you don't know how to find bias, if you
don't know what you're doing in terms of picking a direction that makes sense for your trades that has the
highest degree of probability behind it on a higher time frame, it doesn't guarantee that you're going to be
profitable in every single trade just because you're trading this macro in mind. Macro in the sense that it's
the macro time frame, not a macro that's a time-oriented script algorithmically, that's not what I'm referring
to. But you have to submit yourself to an idea that is larger than the time frame you're trading, because if
you do that, you're using a time element in your trading that is rooted in and founded on a larger time frame
bias that large institutions are actually going to promote, being the weekly and the daily chart. And large
firms, large lending institutions, banks, their interest in the marketplace are not looking at time frames that
we do lots of entries and exits on. They're looking at weekly, monthly, and daily time frames because that's
where the pools of liquidity are going to be located. That's where the inefficiencies are going to be located,
which is the reason why I'm teaching you this year to focus this on these two time frames, even though
you're kicking and screaming, wanting me to go down to the lower time frames to show you these Forex
pairs. You don't need that. Just use what I'm teaching you with the index futures, because it's the same
thing that I'm applying to that market. You're going to do the same things on these currency pairs. It's the
same, it's the same thing, folks.
Price is going to go up for buy side liquidity, or it's going to go up to trade into and reprice to in an
inefficiency. Price is either going to drop down for sell stop or sell side liquidity, or it's going down to reprice
to an inefficiency below price action. That's the only two functions that price does. Anything apart from that
is going to be either consolidation, waiting for sentiment to build up, and then markets will run to where the
majority of the liquidity would be, so it's advantageous for smart money to pair with them. It's all rigged. It's
all premeditated. It's all designed. Anything apart from those three things is manual intervention. That
means that's the real underlying risk. That means that no one knows what's going to happen at that
moment. You have a Black Swan event, an unexpected price reaction. I'll say it with my tongue in cheek
when a news event comes in or market driver releases data, and it should cause the Dollar to go down, but
the Dollar goes up like full force. That's a manual intervention. But they'll always after the fact say it was 'oh,
they discounted, there was already priced in the move,' but they didn't say that beforehand. They're always
going to have something to say after the fact to keep you from seeing that these markets are absolutely
100 percent controlled. They're rigged. Countries will not put their existence and their financial stability in
the hands of speculators. Okay? I'm sorry. I know George Soros gets blamed for, 'Oh, he took, he single
- handedly took down the Pound.' No, he didn't. He's the face. He's the face of something, so it takes your
eyes away from the fact that these are absolutely 100 percent marionette puppets. There's strings on them,
and they dance whenever they want them to. And I'm just teaching you where the strings are attached and
where that marionette's going to place its next foot. That's all I'm showing you, okay?

- RETRACEMENT AND TRADING PSYCHOLOGY
Drop down to a daily chart with this insight in mind. I mean, I wish I had this stuff when I was coming up. It
was awesome. Someone sitting out here giving it to you for free. No upsell coming, no coupon codes, no
future mentorship, buy me this, buy me that. All right, so we are looking at a daily chart here, and you can
see how prices has absolutely been stunning, beautiful delivery. Look how many days it's been down. And
then remember, we're in this inefficiency on the weekly chart, so it would be completely reasonable for it to
try to make an attempt to, you know, retrace a little bit. I'm not suggesting that it should or that it has to. I'm
just saying how do I know? Because I get questioned a lot. 'How do I know when I'm in a trade that it might
start to potentially retrace against me, not necessarily reverse, but how can I avoid having to sit through a
larger retracement that I'm comfortable with?' Well, you can be in partials by now. That way you have
something scaled out in the event that I'm wrong, in the event that you're wrong by holding it for lower if
you're short. You want to always try to find a way to fund your account consistently and not look at it as you
have to have the full pool on every trade. That means from entry to target or term, and that's where you
think the price is ultimately going to go. In the beginning, it's unrealistic; it's unreasonable for you to
anticipate and expect that you are going to be able to do that every single time. And it's not failure for you
not to be able to do that. It's progress reaching towards that goal, and the amount of time that it's going to
take for you to get there, you don't know how much that is going to require of you. I don't know that, and
that's why I can't tell anybody that it's going to be this much time for you to learn how to do this. Everyone's
going to come to this adventure with their own problems, their own character flaws, and you have to wrestle
through them. I can't fix it for you. There's no other person out there that's teaching, and I don't care if
they're wildly profitable and they do live stream and they show you everything, they can't fix these things
that are in us as humans. We have to subdue them ourselves, and that's going to take an unknown amount
of time for each of us. For me, I had it figured out in three, about three, little less than three and a half
years, but I tinkered with it, so it took me six years to know exactly what I was doing. Are you willing to
subscribe to an idea and a pursuit for multiple years and not feel comfortable with the consistency but still
keep doing it? If you do have that wherewithal and that tenacity, then you have the base element that's
required to be a trader because there has to be a level of tenacity that's unshakable. No matter what, you're
going to stay with this. You're going to stick with it because you know it's meant for you, which is different
between a gambler that just doesn't want to leave the table. There's statistical probabilities that I'm sharing,
I'm teaching, improving through third-party audited settings. Now, other people around the world are doing
the same thing. There's no excuse why you don't want to submit the time.

section_title: MITIGATION BLOCKS AND THREE PD ARRAY RULE
content:
You can learn this. You can use whatever time frame that fits your personal life. So don't think that you have
to do it in one specific time frame or one specific model. Every model isn't for everyone. I'm going to say
that again: every single thing that I teach isn't equipped as a perfect glove-in-hand type of marriage where
it fits perfectly for you. It's not for everyone. So it's a wrestling match for you to decide how you're going to
use the information, and I have provided complete models step by step: do this, do this, do this if you want
to trade like this model. And I've coached, I've told you, I've told my private mentorship students exactly
how to use these things to plug and play. You don't need a fair value gap to be the model. You can use a
mitigation block, which I don't talk very much about, but I love them.
What is that? It's this candle right here. This high didn't go above this high. I don't need to because I'm
looking for it to go where? Lower. So inside of this candle here, as price is trading down, it trades down,
trades through it the next day. We're trading in it. Inside this range, you can be a seller right in here,
especially if it's opening and it's trading above it. You're trading in the highest portion of the Power of Three
on a potentially bearish day, in a sell program where the market is likely to keep going lower. Where? Down
here, where I said it was going to go. So inside of this candle, in that range, above the opening of that day,
you could be a short seller.
Risk has to be above the mean threshold of this candle. Why that one? Because the mean threshold is just
above this inefficiency here. So what do I have? Three PD arrays. I have the previous mean threshold of
this down-closed candle. If I'm going to sell short on here using a mitigation block, and I have the mean
threshold of the bearish order block, and I have the high of this old inefficiency. That's three PD arrays. So
my stops are always going to be behind three PD arrays, because if three PD arrays fail, next thing's my
ass. And I don't, I don't want to be in a trade that has three things that I would suggest is helpful for the
trade to be there. If three of them are mixed, that means they're trading through it. That means me as the
operator, me the technician, the mechanic, the person behind the decision of pressing a button, getting into
a trade, that means I have done it wrong. It doesn't mean my system or methodology or my logic is flawed.
It means that I have made the error. There's nothing to wrestle with. There's nothing to argue about.
There's no shame in it. It's absolutely time to get out or have a stop loss in its place so that way there's
nothing to worry about. It's an objective decision that's outside my control. It's going to do its job on paying
for that stop to pay me at that point to get out with a limited amount of loss or whatever profit it allows me to
leave with. How is that for logic? But you're all wrestling with putting a stop loss in because you don't know
how to read price. You have to do the things I'm telling you to do. Avoid the things I'm telling you to avoid.
Focus on learning. Allow yourself to journal. Take these examples. Go back through price action and see if
they don't repeat. And by doing that, what will happen is you'll grow in your understanding. Your stop losses
that have to be wide in the beginning will start to narrow over time. You'll grow more comfortable with not
expecting some wild spiky move, 'Oh my goodness, I'm in a trade. I just know it's going to move 80 pips
against me because that would kill me.' That would be the worst case scenario. And that's how you trade all
the time. You think the boogeyman is going to get you every single time. I'm the boogeyman. I'm telling you
what we do. This is how it's done. There's nothing for you to be afraid of. And if you take a loss, that's not,
that's not being defeated. That's you using your insurance. That's it. You're paying for insurance. You're
paying that stop to ensure that you will not have a catastrophic loss. That's what you want. That's exactly
what you want it to do. But you're looking at it and you're teaching yourself to view a stop loss as 'this is
scary, I don't want this to happen. Let me do everything to avoid it by not using a stop loss,' and then you
wonder why you're wrecking yourself. You're blowing out your accounts. You're drawing down to a point that
you can't come out of. In all manner of drawdown, it can come out. You can come out of that. There's no
drawdown that you can't come out of. It just means that you have to submit to a whole lot of time. And when
you see things like Steve Burns putting up things on Twitter, 'you know, if you do this much drawdown, it
takes you this...' I don't even think about that. I don't look at those things because when you look at things
like that, it says, 'you have to do this much to get your money back if you lose this much in drawdown.'
That's nonsense. Every single series of winning trades is just one incremental step forward. It doesn't
matter how much drawdown you go into. It doesn't matter in the beginning when you're learning. All of
those things are perfect learning experiences. But if you constantly feed yourself a negative diet that if I do
this, this is what's going to happen, and it's always a terrible, most fearful thing for you to have, and you
wonder why you're afraid to trade with a model. You're afraid to use a stop loss. You're afraid to use realistic
leverage, because everybody else is telling you to do ass-backwards theology. Think, folks. Think. How
many times do I have to tell you something before it happens? How long does it have to happen? How
many more students do you have to see come forward with real results? And you have to not be selective
in your hearing when I'm teaching and I'm telling you what to focus on and how to think about it. Try to
apply, it's going to be uncomfortable probably, but try very hard to try to apply it to how you study price. And
that's the only, it's a growing pain. You have to go through these things to learn what not to do and focus on
the things that you've done right and cheerlead yourself. When you did it right, really pour it on thick in your
journal. 'I'm so pleased. I'm so proud of myself that I was able to see this coming. I'm glad I did not hurt
myself with this idea that if I would have looked at it this way, this would have resulted in a loss, but I didn't
see it this way. I saw it this way.' You're encouraging yourself. You're being your own counselor, your own
best cheerleader, and you're writing the best technical analysis book in trading literature there is because
it's applicable to you. Anybody else that would have their hands on it, reading it, wouldn't get the same
thing from it. But you, when you read it, you'll be able to refer back to these milestones where you've grown
in your understanding, and you encouraged yourself through rough patches. And you're going to have to
have that. There's going to be a period where you are encountering the market, and my personal students,
you know that paid me, we had periods where I just simply didn't want to do anything, and it was
uncomfortable for some of them because they're like, 'We paid you. You should be able to tell us.' 'Yeah, I
told you to sit still. Sit still and wait.' And then when the market cleared itself up and moved out of an
uncertainty, a consolidation that was not conducive for them to learn well in, I'm right back to 90 plus strike
right again. And again, my students would have came forward with proof that that wasn't true if it wasn't. All
right, so let's go into.

section_title: AUSSIE DOLLAR AND GOLD MARKET ANALYSIS
content:
I wanted to look at Aussie dollar real quick as I mentioned briefly. Look at that. All right, so right away, right
under here, admittedly, I thought we would get this low taken out while I was sleeping last night. So just be
mindful that there's sell side below here, so I would like to see it, and over the course of today and going
into Friday, I'd like to see it gravitate below that. I'm not saying that that's all there is, but to me it makes
sense for it to reach down there. Bullish dollar, bearish everything else, basically.
Gold. Why don't you talk about gold, ICT? Because gold is a market that is very, very manipulated, very
manipulated. So, um, can you trade it? Can I trade it? Yes. Do my students trade it? Yes. Is it something
you should try to trade every single day? I don't believe so. But that's my opinion, and if you have a model,
if you have an approach, or if your whole existence as a trader revolves around this, you know, what is my
opinion? Nothing. It's just my opinion. It's like everybody has one. So if we look at this gap in here, I don't
recall exactly when I said it, but obviously it's in the chart, so I know I talked about gold eventually trying to
reach down into the inefficiency, which is here. So I'm going to leave it to you all then to look at your notes
or go back and, you know, sift through all the videos. It's been commentary since, since here, about in this
time frame, so I guess late May, early June. From that time frame, I was talking about how we could
potentially trail down into this, this gap in here, and take out the sell side below here. We got real close to it
there. So it, it, to me it makes perfect sense if Dollar keeps going higher, that's going to put pressure on, I'm
not saying selling pressure, but it's going to put pressure on the sentiment idea of gold wanting to be
bullish. So higher Dollar, that's going to allow for weaker prices in Gold. Two diametrically opposed, when
they're not and they move in tandem, you're in a very, very risky scenario. That's a decoupling. So when
there's a decoupling, that means you have to be a scalper or do nothing. So we have the low here and the
low here, so they're relatively equal, and you have the inefficiency that's open. And if it accelerates below
this low here, we can trade down into the order block that begins at this candle's opening. So those are the
levels that I'd be interested in as long as Gold, I'm sorry, as long as the Dollar is going higher. Those, those
are the levels I'm looking for, which is below these lows here. A complete repricing of this, this candle's high
on March 10th, in the low of this candle on March 14th, that inefficiency which is shaded in blue, I would
like to see that entirely closed and reprice to, assuming Dollar's going higher still. And then if it accelerates
lower down into the order block right here.

section_title: GOLD BEARISH STANCE AND INVALIDATION CRITERIA
content:
Okay, so I'm not bullish on gold. I would not be going short. I don't want to trade it, but I know a lot of you
ask me, so I've now commented and told you what I believe. What changes this? What would change my
constitution of what I believe that gold may or may not do? Gold would have to go above this order block
right there on a closing basis. So as long as we're not closing above September 22nd, that candle's high,
which is 1929 (we'll just call it 1929 and a half), as long as we don't give a closing on a daily chart above
that order block, I believe that these levels here are in play for gold, and I think that's pretty clear. I'm not in
it. I'm not going to trade it. But for the sake of the folks that are asking, that's the, that's the line in the sand
that I believe that we're reaching for, the three of them here. And I believe that it is the icebreaker that
changes the whole discussion, and I'm wrong if it goes on a closing basis above this candle's high. Okay?
So as long as that's intact in price action, that's how I'm viewing gold. So we can go forth with that. And let's
go to ES real quick. This is already longer than I wanted it to be. Some are like, 'Who cares, stay on all day,
ICT, I'm here. I'm here for it.' I am not here for that. Let's see what time it is real quick. Nine o'clock. I got a
couple minutes left, and then I have to sit with my son here. Part of his homeschooling is reading price
action with me. You like that? All right, so let's go through the weekly chart. Get through this real quick.
Again, if you're learning today, if you like what you're hearing, seeing, just give it a thumbs up. It's free.
Doesn't cost you anything. I promise no coupon codes are required.

- QUARTERLY SHIFT AND VOLUME IMBALANCE
All right, so if you recall, as we were trading in August, I said that we were going to go up and trade into this
high here for ES. And on the NASDAQ, I'll show you the same thing. There's a small little gap in here. I said
we would run that, and then I said, thinking in terms of a quarterly shift (that means the next two to three
months), 'What am I expecting?' Because everybody likes to think that I cherry pick things and delete
things that weren't accurate. That's not the case. I never delete anything. The idea of a quarterly shift is
absolutely unavoidable in terms of whether I'm right or wrong. So, I said that we would go up to go down.
Did we take out that high? No, we traded just inside this inefficiency right there. Then what I said the
quarterly shift, I'm expecting. I want to see as we see price trading into October, November, I think that's
verbatim as I can get. I know I said going into October, but my students and my core content lessons teach
you this, so it's a matter of listening to what I taught, I'm putting into practice. I did the analysis on YouTube.
It was in live stream. It was in recorded sessions that are time and date stamped. You can't escape it. It's
there. And here we are. We're trading inside the inefficiency down here. I mentioned that inside this
inefficiency between this weekly low candle, coming in at 4352.5, and this candle here on May 22nd of
2023, I have it drawn down to this body. The reason why I did that, and I explained it (I don't know if it was
in a Twitter commentary video or if I've done something on recording on YouTube), I'll leave it to you. And if
you know which one I'm referring to, where I talked about how I'm just going to draw it down to this area
because it takes into account the volume imbalance that's here. Like if I take this off for a second, right
away your eye should snap right to that as a volume imbalance. So usually, you would see me do this
(draws on chart) and look where it stopped: consequent encroachment. Because there's no bodies there,
there's no balanced price range, there's no order block. So the midpoint is what? Consequent
encroachment. So let's test the theory from body to body. What's the price here? 4307 and a quarter. What
is the low here? 4305 and a half. And we're looking at a weekly chart.
Now if you were using analysis concepts on a weekly chart and you were wanting to see if there was
something that's measurable in terms of precision, if there's something, there's a rhyme and reason behind
why price should do something, that should be a very good selling point that what I'm teaching you on a
higher grade time frame, like weekly charts, like there's a whole lot of price action, a whole lot of
randomness, a whole lot of buying and selling pressure going on. Of course, I'm saying that facetiously, but
that's pretty tight. No matter how you slice it, where you come from, what school of thought, that's a pretty
good estimation for where a realistic draw on liquidity should be. Because if you think that it's only going
down here to go higher, I don't subscribe to that view. I believe that we might want to get into this a little bit
more and reprice to the entire close of that. That's what I'm willing to subscribe to going into October. I
don't, I don't think we've hit the low yet. I don't think we've seen capitulation. I don't think that we're bullish
longer term. We're going into the harder part of times for the marketplace, in my opinion. But this is what
the volume imbalance would look like if it was there in the chart. But because it was not, and I had shown
you this, I incorporated the entire scope of the range between what would otherwise be drawn like this.
That would be a fair value gap in the form of a buy side imbalance sell side inefficiency. But because we
have this volume imbalance, that's why I said I would draw it to here, so that encapsulates all of it. So it
kind of like gives you a little bit more detail. But here we are. We're trading down here. Go back and watch
the August commentaries where I said it was going to go up here, and then the quarterly shift is coming,
drawing down into there. So how many handles is that? 300-ish on the S&P, by the way, which is, that's a
significant price run.

- BREAKAWAY GAP AND PD ARRAY CONFLUENCE
Let's go into the daily chart. I say this stuff to remind you and to have an emotional experience by helping
you remember that these things were said to you beforehand. That's not bragging. I know how to brag. This
is not bragging. It's to make you understand that these things were shared to you and with you real time, in
a capacity that can't be faked. So the market ran up in here, took out the high, broke lower, traded higher
inefficiency, bearish order block right there. Price breaks. We want to see what? We want to see proof that
it wants to go lower. What would that look like? A breakaway gap right there. Did that fill in? Did this fill in?
Did it even reprice to it? No. So this is a good signature to have to anticipate that we're really strongly in
contention to run these lows out here, and we did. So come back up. Trade into this low. Why is this low
critical? 'Classic support resistance, guys,' because now you're thinking, 'Oh, well, he's making this
complicated because all I would do is sell short here.' Okay, show me you entered short there. If you know
that this is how it works for the folks that come to me in my comment section and saying it's this and it's
that, show me your trade entry on that stuff. You only talk about after it happens. I'm talking about before it
happens, and then I'm trading with real money on it. Hello. Why is this low now supposedly a support after
support broken now becomes resistance? That's the theory, right? That's what everybody learns in the
books. Why did it work right here? Why did that happen? Has nothing to do with this low. What's over here?
Study that. Everything in here gives you this and that. Price runs down, falls short of what? The imbalance
on the weekly chart that you can't see here, can't see it in here. Remember, this is the range that's drawn
on the weekly chart, this, that's this right here. So if we go back to the daily, if we wanted to do the
inefficiency on this time frame, start with here. There, and utilizing the order block. But we have what
showing on that? We have a close below it, meaning, and I take that as, 'Okay, it might want to reprice up
into what's already been delivered,' which is this down-closed candle. And then we have what next to it
right there? A wick. So inside of this wick, there's halfway, which is consequent encroachment. So I would
have that level on your chart. I'm not going to do it because I don't want to spend time doing it. I'm going to
get through it, but I just want to tell you where they are. You want to have that level on your chart because it
might and it could very easily reach up into that and make everybody think that the low is in place, and then
just use that as a reason to trade lower. I'd like to personally, I would like to see it not trade there because
the fact that it's not trading there makes it a PD array as well, because it would be treated the same way I
would view like this breakaway gap up here. A PD array is not traded to when I am allowing for and my
algorithm allows for it to trade to it normally. If it doesn't do that, the information and the insight that it gives
is that means that I am absolutely bearish. That means the market is really bearish if it can't trade up into
consequent encroachment on that wick. But if it does, it would need to do what? It would need to
completely smash the out of it and keep it from going any higher because if this gives up the ghost, think
about what we have. We have mean threshold of this down-closed candle, which I'll, um, I'll do it here for
the sake of completion. We have half of that range, which is 4343.75. Then we have the range of that
inefficiency on the weekly chart on the, on here. You want precision. These are the things you got to do,
folks. This is too much. It's a complication, okay? Trading with mediocrity. Then you have mean threshold
here. So right away, we have an agreement that there's two PD arrays saying that this is reasonable to
trade to, but can it go beyond that in just like a Mohawk or or stab up there and still remain bearish? What
would we have to contend with? It would be this. Not bad (drawing on chart). It's this. Why I keep grabbing
that? Good grief, ICT. If you would have just paid attention, you were grabbing the wrong thing. I know,
armchair quarterbacks. So 4351.75. Okay, so in my mind, it's perfectly acceptable. It's permissible. It's
reasonable. It would not change the bearish tone that the market has. It can trade that much higher today,
tomorrow, Friday, into next week and still be bearish for me. Now, if we get a daily close above that 4351
and then three quarters, that's problematic, because why? That's three PD arrays that it's now going
through. Oh, light bulbs are coming on, aren't they? But this is not even a weekly chart. So if you would
have did this, there's nothing wrong with that. Absolutely nothing wrong with it. But you can't just simply just
use that when you have a real, um, volume imbalance down here. There's, there's a, a link between this
candle and this candle only by the basis of its wick. See that? This little wick here. So you have to, you have
to encapsulate that too, just like I mentioned on the weekly chart. So if there's ever a gap or inefficiency like
a volume imbalance within a buy side or sell side imbalance or fair value gap, you have to incorporate the
entirety of that because it's going to be meaningful. It's going to be impactful as you can see. It's even went
a little bit outside that there. And then this order block here, you can use the last down-closed (up), I'm
sorry, the last down-closed candle prior to it moving higher. That is the change in the state of delivery. That
candle's opening price. So you can incorporate that as well. So those are the key levels I would have for
ES, and I'm not changing my stance on, I believe we're going lower still, um, and I just told you exactly what
would change my mind about being bearish. Okay? It would have to close on the daily chart above the
mean threshold, time being mean threshold, consequent encroachment of that candlestick's tail or wick
under the body of it. And now we can move over to NASDAQ.

- VOLUME IMBALANCE AND BEARISH INVALIDATION
Finally made it. We finally made it here. The chart. I got a couple minutes. I can do it. I can do it. All right, so
here is the same application of what I was referring to through this lecture. If you're using inefficiencies and
mapping them on your chart, if you have a volume imbalance that's part of what would be the candles that
make the buy side imbalance sell side efficiency, in this case here, the fair value gap with an up close,
which is a busy, you can't ignore the volume imbalance. You have to include that if you really want to see
the total scope of the inefficiency. That's how you would do it. You don't want to just do it this way because
you're not making or taking into account that it could trade down there too. Okay, so that's why it's there.
And we have seen it trade down into the volume imbalance here, and consequent encroachment of this
wick. See that? So we went below here for sell side. It's perfectly reasonable for it to trade above this
candle's high and trade into this portion here. I would not want to see it close above the volume imbalance
that's right there. As long as we don't close above the volume imbalance right here, I'm bearish long term.
That makes, that changes the whole setting for me. But it can trade all the way up and it can touch. It can
trade into it, but it cannot close above that. If it closes above that, I am not bearish. Doesn't mean I'm going
long, but it doesn't mean anything but I've abandoned bearishness because I think that ultimately we're
going to see before Thanksgiving (showing a specific low on the chart). That's where I think NASDAQ's
going to be before Turkey Day, barring any run on a closing basis here, above that weekly volume
imbalance.
Okay, why am I picking that? Because we have this week's high. That's a PD array. We have... You guys are
really learning some stuff today. I really expect to see some measure of thumbs up on this because it really,
it discourages me that you're willing to sit through this stuff for free. I'm not charging anything. I could be
done. I got my son living, waiting here next to me, looking at me like, you know, 'Are we going to be doing
anything around this? Going to watch this?' Like, I have other things I could be doing, and I'm asking for the
simplest little currency, which is a thumbs up. It'll help you find this video also because it'll be in your liked
videos. Here's another thing. You can create a YouTube channel as a student of mine and like certain
things, and it'll allow you to go to those very videos to have those certain things that you want to revisit.
